text.title: Retroactive ADR Workflow Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

conclusion.verdict: set status to accepted-retroactively; otherwise it pollutes the forward-ADR history

branch.question: Retro-ADR body contains pejorative reference to a named ex-employee?

conclusion.verdict: rewrite using passive voice or system-level subject; remove blame fields
park excess; resume weekly cadence with top-3-by-priority next session
pick one owner; the other writes a separate retro-ADR or contributes via `alternatives_considered_now`

branch.question: Decision is old enough that no original-intent evidence remains?

conclusion.verdict: downgrade to `reconstructed-retroactively` sub-status; mark intent unrecoverable instead of pretending recovery
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
